description: 20-question benchmark for Flat RAG vs GraphRAG over the Tech/EV Company Corpus. Each item has gold `keywords` used for automatic recall scoring (fraction of gold facts present in the system answer/context). `type` marks single-hop (fact in one chunk) vs multi-hop (needs connecting >=2 facts/entities).

questions.question: How many new electric vehicles did Americans buy in the first quarter of 2024?
questions.reference: About 268,909 new EVs were bought in Q1 2024 (Kelley Blue Book counts).

questions.question: What was the EV share of total new-vehicle sales in the US in Q1 2024?
questions.reference: 7.3% of new-vehicle sales were electric in Q1 2024.

questions.question: By how much did Tesla's US sales fall year-over-year in Q1 2024?
questions.reference: Tesla's US sales were down about 13.3% year over year.

questions.question: What was Tesla's share of the US electric vehicle market in Q1 2024, and what was it a year earlier?
questions.reference: Tesla's EV market share was 51.3% in Q1 2024, down from 61.7% one year earlier.

questions.question: What was the average transaction price for a new EV in the US in Q1 2024?
questions.reference: The average new-EV transaction price was $55,167 in Q1 2024.

questions.question: What is the full federal EV incentive amount offered by the Inflation Reduction Act?
questions.reference: The Inflation Reduction Act offers a full $7,500 incentive.

questions.question: How many US electric vehicles were sold annually from 2018 to 2020?
questions.reference: More than 315,000 EVs were sold annually from 2018 to 2020.

questions.question: Which firm's sales counts did Cox Automotive rely on when analyzing US EV sales?
questions.reference: Cox Automotive used Kelley Blue Book counts.

questions.question: Which Chinese EV maker held a strong US IPO that improved investor sentiment toward Chinese-linked stocks?
questions.reference: Zeekr, the Hangzhou-based premium EV maker, had a strong US IPO.

questions.question: Which US automaker set a 2035 goal to phase out internal combustion engines but later pulled back from its 400,000 EV production goal?
questions.reference: General Motors announced a goal to phase out internal combustion engines by 2035 and pulled away from its 400,000 EV goal by mid-2024.

questions.question: General Motors cancelled plans to build affordable EVs with which partner?
questions.reference: GM cancelled plans to pair with Honda to build affordable EVs.

questions.question: What US President declared an intention to revoke the electric vehicle mandate and end the Green New Deal?
questions.reference: Donald Trump declared he would revoke the EV mandate and end the Green New Deal.

questions.question: Roughly what fraction of all EVs were leased in Q1 2024?
questions.reference: About 27% of all EVs were leased in Q1 2024.

questions.question: What year-over-year EV sales increase did Ford achieve in Q1 2024?
questions.reference: Ford achieved an 86.1% year-over-year increase in Q1 EV sales.

questions.question: Which affordable Chevrolet EV had its sales temporarily halted, with a new version expected in 2025?
questions.reference: The Chevy Bolt had sales halted, with a new version expected in 2025.

questions.question: Name three of the nine manufacturers that recorded more than 50% year-over-year EV sales growth in Q1 2024.
questions.reference: BMW, Cadillac, Ford, Hyundai, Kia, Lexus, Mercedes, Rivian and Vinfast recorded >50% growth.

questions.question: What EV share of the US market did Cox Automotive forecast by the end of 2024?
questions.reference: Cox Automotive forecast roughly 10% EV share by the end of 2024.

questions.question: Which single Tesla model was responsible for almost half of all US EV sales around 2019?
questions.reference: The Tesla Model 3 was responsible for almost half of all US EV sales.

questions.question: What was Tesla's average transaction price in Q1 2024 and how did it compare year over year?
questions.reference: Tesla's average transaction price was $52,315 in Q1 2024, down roughly 13.5% year over year.

questions.question: What US legislation ignited a major investment cycle in battery factories but limited the brands qualifying for the full EV credit?
questions.reference: The Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) ignited battery-factory investment and limited qualifying brands for the $7,500 credit.
